experiment: Q6_K 8-lane AVX2 row dot (CAMELID_X86_Q6K_AVX2) vs the scalar oracle, CPU K-quant decode
host: i7-11800H (16T, Tiger Lake AVX2+AVX-512) / Win11 / 15.7 GiB DDR4
cpu_lane: CUDA hidden (CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=-1, CAMELID_CUDA_RESIDENT_{DECODE,PREFILL}=0); K-quant CPU decode default-on
model: Qwen3-4B-Q4_K_M.gguf (Q6_K rows: attn_v, ffn_down, tied lm_head)

parity.evidence: output_token_ids SHA-256 == 777cf33dd34d for ALL iterations, flag OFF and ON. Unit-proven by q6_k_wire_row_dot_avx2_bit_identical (bit-for-bit vs the 8-lane scalar oracle across nblk=1/2/5/11).

verdict: REGRESSION (-21% median, 6.34 -> 5.00 tok/s). The hand-written AVX2 (128-bit cvtepi8->mullo16->extract128->mullo32) is SLOWER than the autovectorized scalar oracle, which the x86-64-v3 + AVX-512 build already vectorizes well; and CPU decode is DRAM-bandwidth-bound (the 6-bit rebuild + weight reads dominate, not the integer dot). Same pattern as the Q8 gated-SIMD regression (-8..-11%) and the Phase-3 prefetch null.
decision: CAMELID_X86_Q6K_AVX2 stays DEFAULT-OFF. The kernel + bit-identical test are retained as (a) proof the 8-lane order is AVX2-reproducible bit-for-bit, (b) a parity-guarded regression guard, (c) ready for a non-bandwidth-bound host where it may help. NOT promoted.
note: This closes the conductor's Q6_K-AVX2 ask: the kernel exists, is parity-proven, and is measured — and the measurement says do not enable it on this box. The honest-null/negative IS the deliverable.
